name: Remboursements et dégrèvements d'impôts d'État
created: 2006 (LOLF implementation; programme created with the new budget framework under loi organique n°2001-692 du 1er août 2001)
description: Covers mandatory refunds, tax credits, and abatements on State taxes (income tax, corporate tax, VAT). This is an evaluative-credit programme that adjusts automatically based on taxpayer claims and legislative entitlements.
beneficiaries: Approximately 38-40 million tax households and all VAT-registered businesses in France. The programme does not 'serve' beneficiaries in a social sense; it processes fiscal corrections owed by the State.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Remboursements et dégrèvements; DGFiP annual reports.

mission: Avances aux collectivités territoriales (compte de concours financiers)
name: Avances sur le montant des impositions revenant aux régions, départements, communes, établissements et divers organismes
created: 2006 (LOLF framework; transfers from former Treasury advance mechanisms dating to the 1950s-60s)
description: Provides monthly cash advances to local authorities (communes, departments, regions, EPCI) on the proceeds of local taxes collected by the State on their behalf, before final settlement. This ensures continuous funding of local government operations.
beneficiaries: Approximately 35,000 communes, 101 departments, 18 regions, and thousands of EPCIs and other local bodies across metropolitan and overseas France.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Compte de concours financiers 'Avances aux collectivités territoriales'; DGCL data.

name: Pensions civiles et militaires de retraite
created: 2006 (CAS Pensions created by LOLF; pension regime itself governed by Code des pensions civiles et militaires, originally ordonnance of 1945 consolidated in 1964)
description: Finances retirement pensions for civil servants (État) and military personnel. Funded by employer contributions from ministries and employee payroll deductions, managed as a special-purpose account (CAS).
beneficiaries: Approximately 2.6 million retirees and their surviving beneficiaries (about 1.6 million former civil servants and 1 million military pensioners and reversionary rights holders).
source_notes: PLF 2024 - CAS Pensions; Service des retraites de l'État (SRE) annual report; Cour des comptes pension reports.

name: Charge de la dette et trésorerie de l'État
created: 2006 (LOLF framework; debt management previously under various Treasury budget lines. Agence France Trésor created in 2001)
description: Covers interest payments on government debt (OATs, BTF, inflation-indexed bonds) and cash management costs. Managed by the Agence France Trésor (AFT) under the Ministry of Finance.
beneficiaries: Not applicable (no direct individual beneficiaries). Bondholders are institutional investors, foreign central banks, insurance companies, and retail investors holding French sovereign debt.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Engagements financiers de l'État; AFT monthly bulletins; Cour des comptes annual certification.

name: Enseignement scolaire public du second degré
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; public secondary education organised by lois Ferry 1881-82, expanded by réforme Haby 1975 and loi d'orientation 1989)
description: Funds public secondary education (collèges and lycées), including teacher salaries, pedagogical support staff, and educational operations for grades 6 through terminale across metropolitan and overseas France.
beneficiaries: Approximately 5.6 million students enrolled in public secondary schools (about 3.4 million in collèges and 2.2 million in lycées généraux, technologiques et professionnels).
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Enseignement scolaire; DEPP (Direction de l'évaluation, de la prospective et de la performance) statistical yearbook.

name: Enseignement scolaire public du premier degré
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; primary education framework from lois Ferry 1881-82, loi Goblet 1886, updated by loi d'orientation 2013)
description: Funds public primary education (écoles maternelles and élémentaires), covering teacher salaries, training, and pedagogical supervision for children aged 3 to 11.
beneficiaries: Approximately 5.7 million students in public primary schools (about 2.5 million in maternelle and 3.2 million in élémentaire), plus approximately 350,000 teachers.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Enseignement scolaire; DEPP annual statistics; Cour des comptes education reports.

name: Soutien de la politique de la défense
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; consolidates defence support functions previously spread across service budgets. Restructured under LPM 2019-2025)
description: Funds transversal support functions for the Ministry of Armed Forces: human resources, infrastructure, information systems, real estate management, and general administration. It is the ministry's backbone programme.
beneficiaries: Approximately 270,000 military and civilian personnel of the Ministry of Armed Forces benefit from the HR, infrastructure, and IT services funded by this programme.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Défense; LPM 2024-2030 (loi n°2023-703); Ministry of Armed Forces annual performance reports.

name: Équipement des forces
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; major equipment procurement historically under Délégation générale pour l'armement, DGA, created 1961)
description: Finances the acquisition, development, and maintenance of major military equipment systems: combat aircraft (Rafale), submarines (Barracuda), armoured vehicles (Scorpion/Griffon), satellites, and naval vessels.
beneficiaries: Not applicable in the individual sense. Serves the three armed services (Armée de terre, Marine nationale, Armée de l'air et de l'espace) and the defence industrial base (Dassault, Naval Group, MBDA, Thales, etc.).
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Défense; LPM 2024-2030; DGA annual activity reports; Cour des comptes defence equipment audits.

name: Aide à l'accès au logement
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; APL system created by loi n°77-1 du 3 janvier 1977, reformed by loi ELAN 2018 and real-time calculation reform 2021)
description: Funds personal housing allowances (APL, ALS, ALF) that subsidize rents and mortgage payments for low-income households, students, and families. Since 2021, benefits are calculated on real-time income (contemporaneous basis).
beneficiaries: Approximately 6.5 million households (around 13 million individuals) receiving one of the three forms of housing aid, managed by CAF/MSA.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Cohésion des territoires; CNAF statistical reports; Cour des comptes housing aid audit 2022.

name: Préparation et emploi des forces
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; operational readiness function, reorganised under the authority of the Chef d'état-major des armées, CEMA)
description: Funds the operational readiness, training, and deployment of French armed forces, including OPEX (external operations), domestic operations (Sentinelle), exercises, and day-to-day activity of military units.
beneficiaries: Approximately 200,000 active military personnel across all services who are trained and deployed under this programme, plus forces engaged in external operations (Sahel, Indo-Pacific, etc.).
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Défense; Cour des comptes OPEX reports; Senate defence committee hearings.

name: Accompagnement des mutations économiques et développement de l'emploi
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; policy tools include contrats aidés dating to 1977, formation professionnelle framework from loi Delors 1971, updated by loi Avenir professionnel 2018)
description: Funds employment support measures including subsidized contracts, apprenticeship incentives, professional training, business competitiveness clusters, and support for workers affected by economic restructuring.
beneficiaries: Several million workers and jobseekers annually. Key sub-programmes: approximately 800,000 apprenticeship contracts, hundreds of thousands of subsidized employment contracts (PEC, CIE), and professional training beneficiaries.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Travail et emploi; DARES studies; France compétences annual report.

mission: Solidarité, insertion et égalité des chances
name: Handicap et dépendance
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; AAH created by loi n°75-534 du 30 juin 1975; MDPH framework from loi handicap n°2005-102 du 11 février 2005)
description: Funds the Allocation aux adultes handicapés (AAH) and related disability benefits, support for disabled workers in ESAT establishments, and contributions to the care of dependent persons in specialised facilities.
beneficiaries: Approximately 1.3 million AAH recipients, plus around 120,000 workers in ESAT (établissements et services d'aide par le travail), and beneficiaries of other disability-related aids.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Solidarité; CNAF-AAH statistics; DREES études et résultats; IGAS inspection reports.

mission: Solidarité, insertion et égalité des chances
name: Inclusion sociale et protection des personnes
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; includes the prime d'activité created by loi n°2015-994, RSA recentralisation experiments, and protection juridique des majeurs)
description: Funds the prime d'activité (in-work benefit), food aid programmes, protection of vulnerable adults (tutelle/curatelle), and social inclusion measures for the most disadvantaged populations.
beneficiaries: Approximately 4.6 million households receiving the prime d'activité (around 9-10 million individuals), plus beneficiaries of food aid (associations serving 5+ million people) and persons under legal protection (about 800,000).
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Solidarité; CNAF prime d'activité statistics; Cour des comptes social inclusion reports.

name: Police nationale
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; Police nationale created in its modern form by loi n°66-492 du 9 juillet 1966, unified under DGPN. Restructured by LOPMI 2023)
description: Funds the operations, personnel, equipment, and infrastructure of the Police nationale, covering public safety, judicial police investigations, border control (PAF), intelligence (DGSI funded separately), and CRS riot police.
beneficiaries: Not applicable as individual beneficiaries. The programme employs approximately 152,000 police officers and administrative staff, serving the entire population in police zones (urban areas, about 50% of territory covering ~70% of population).
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Sécurités; LOPMI (loi n°2023-22 du 24 janvier 2023); IGPN annual reports.

name: Gendarmerie nationale
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; Gendarmerie nationale traces to the Maréchaussée of 1373, modern statute from loi n°2009-971 du 3 août 2009 rattaching it to the Ministry of Interior)
description: Funds the operations, personnel, equipment, and infrastructure of the Gendarmerie nationale, responsible for public safety, judicial police, and defence missions in rural areas and small towns (95% of national territory).
beneficiaries: Not applicable as individual beneficiaries. Employs approximately 100,000 military gendarmes and 4,000 civilian staff, responsible for security across 95% of French territory covering about 30% of the population.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Sécurités; LOPMI 2023; IGGN annual reports; Senate security committee hearings.

name: Aide économique et financière au développement
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; French development aid policy framed by loi n°2021-1031 du 4 août 2021 de programmation relative au développement solidaire. AFD charter since 1998)
description: Funds bilateral economic and financial development aid, primarily through the Agence française de développement (AFD), including concessional loans, grants to developing countries, contributions to multilateral development banks, and debt relief initiatives (C2D).
beneficiaries: Indirect beneficiaries span populations of partner countries, primarily in Sub-Saharan Africa (19 priority countries designated by the CICID), Southeast Asia, and the Middle East. No single individual beneficiary count applicable.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Aide publique au développement; AFD annual report; OECD DAC peer review of France.

name: Formations supérieures et recherche universitaire
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; university system governed by loi Faure 1968, loi Savary 1984, loi LRU 2007 granting autonomy, loi ORE 2018)
description: Funds French public universities and grandes écoles under the Ministry of Higher Education: operating subsidies, faculty salaries, university research, student-facing infrastructure, and the Parcoursup admissions system.
beneficiaries: Approximately 1.7 million students enrolled in public universities and attached institutions, plus about 90,000 teaching and research staff (enseignants-chercheurs) and 60,000 BIATSS support staff.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Recherche et enseignement supérieur (MIRES); DGESIP data; HCERES evaluations.

name: Recherches scientifiques et technologiques pluridisciplinaires
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; CNRS founded 1939, other EPST/EPIC research bodies created at various dates. Framed by loi de programmation de la recherche 2020)
description: Funds France's major public research organisations (organismes de recherche): CNRS, INSERM, INRIA, CEA (civil), IRD, and others. Covers researcher salaries, laboratory operations, large-scale scientific infrastructure, and ANR (Agence nationale de la recherche) grants.
beneficiaries: Approximately 35,000 researchers and 40,000 support staff across EPST/EPIC bodies, plus thousands of PhD candidates and postdocs funded through ANR project grants. Indirectly serves the entire national research ecosystem.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - MIRES; LPR (loi n°2020-1674 du 24 décembre 2020); HCERES; CNRS annual report.

name: Enseignement privé du premier et du second degrés
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; State funding of private schools under contract governed by loi Debré n°59-1557 du 31 décembre 1959)
description: Funds teacher salaries and operating subsidies for private schools under contract (sous contrat) with the State, both at primary and secondary levels, primarily Catholic schools. The State pays teachers; local authorities contribute to operating costs.
beneficiaries: Approximately 2.1 million students in private schools under contract (about 17% of total school enrolment), and approximately 140,000 teachers paid by the State in these establishments.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Enseignement scolaire; DEPP Repères et références statistiques; Secrétariat général de l'enseignement catholique.

name: Vie de l'élève
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; student welfare policies codified in Code de l'éducation, bourses scolaires system reformed periodically)
description: Funds student welfare services in primary and secondary schools: school health services (médecine scolaire), social workers, school scholarships (bourses), special needs assistants (AESH), and school climate initiatives including anti-bullying programmes.
beneficiaries: Approximately 3 million scholarship recipients (bourses de collège and lycée), around 130,000 AESH accompanying 430,000+ disabled students, plus all students benefiting from health and social services.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Enseignement scolaire; DEPP; IGÉSR inspection reports.

name: Accès et retour à l'emploi
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; Pôle emploi created 2008 merging ANPE+Assedic, transformed into France Travail by loi n°2023-1196 du 18 décembre 2023)
description: Funds the public employment service (France Travail, ex-Pôle emploi), subsidized employment contracts for priority groups, local employment initiatives (missions locales for youth), and insertion through economic activity (IAE structures).
beneficiaries: France Travail registers approximately 5.5 million jobseekers (categories A-E). Missions locales serve about 1.1 million young people aged 16-25. IAE structures employ around 140,000 persons in insertion pathways annually.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Travail et emploi; France Travail (ex-Pôle emploi) annual statistics; DARES indicators.

mission: Amortissement de la dette de l'État liée à la covid-19 (CAS)
name: Amortissement de la dette de l'État liée à la covid-19
created: 2022 (created by loi n°2022-1726 du 30 décembre 2022, LFI 2023; CAS dedicated to repaying the ~165 Md€ of additional debt accumulated during the 2020-2021 pandemic response)
description: Dedicated special account (CAS) for the progressive repayment of Covid-19-related government debt over 20 years (2022-2042). Funded by earmarked revenue from a fraction of corporate and income tax receipts.
beneficiaries: Not applicable (debt amortisation programme). Indirectly benefits all French taxpayers by ring-fencing and scheduling the repayment of pandemic-era borrowing, estimated at 165 billion euros.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - CAS Amortissement dette covid-19; Cour des comptes rapport sur la dette publique; AFT.

name: Hébergement, parcours vers le logement et insertion des personnes vulnérables
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; emergency shelter system codified by loi DALO n°2007-290 du 5 mars 2007 and loi ALUR 2014. Programme significantly expanded since 2015 migrant crisis)
description: Funds emergency shelters (hébergement d'urgence), transitional housing (CHRS), the 115 emergency hotline, accommodation for asylum seekers, and 'Housing First' (Un chez-soi d'abord) programmes for homeless and vulnerable populations.
beneficiaries: Approximately 200,000+ emergency shelter places nightly (record levels since 2020), serving an estimated 300,000+ homeless persons annually, including families, isolated individuals, and persons in irregular situations.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Cohésion des territoires; DIHAL reports; Fondation Abbé Pierre annual report; Cour des comptes hébergement audit.

name: Justice judiciaire
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; judicial organisation governed by Code de l'organisation judiciaire, major reform by loi n°2023-22 du 20 novembre 2023 d'orientation et de programmation de la Justice)
description: Funds the operations of civil and criminal courts (tribunaux judiciaires, cours d'appel, Cour de cassation), including magistrate and clerk salaries, legal aid (aide juridictionnelle), and court infrastructure modernisation.
beneficiaries: Approximately 9,000 magistrates and 24,000 greffiers/fonctionnaires serving the judicial system. Legal aid (aide juridictionnelle) benefits about 1 million litigants annually. The entire justiciable population (68 million residents) is indirectly served.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Justice; LOPJ 2023; Conseil supérieur de la magistrature; Cour des comptes justice reports.

name: Administration pénitentiaire
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; prison administration governed by loi pénitentiaire n°2009-1436 du 24 novembre 2009, reinforced by LOPJ 2023 with 15,000 new prison places target)
description: Funds the operation of prisons (établissements pénitentiaires), probation and reintegration services (SPIP), prison construction/renovation programmes, and healthcare and vocational training for inmates.
beneficiaries: Approximately 74,000 inmates (as of 2024, chronic overcrowding at ~120% capacity in maisons d'arrêt), plus about 170,000 persons under probation/community supervision. Employs approximately 44,000 prison staff (surveillants, CPIP, administrative).
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Justice; DAP monthly statistics; CGLPL (Contrôleur général des lieux de privation de liberté) reports; LOPJ 2023.

name: Urbanisme, territoires et amélioration de l'habitat
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; housing renovation policies from loi SRU 2000, ANAH created 1971, MaPrimeRénov' launched 2020 replacing CITE)
description: Funds urban planning, housing renovation (through ANAH and MaPrimeRénov' energy retrofit grants), social housing production support, and territorial planning operations including ORT (Opérations de revitalisation de territoire).
beneficiaries: MaPrimeRénov' served approximately 670,000 households in 2023. ANAH overall assists about 800,000 households annually. Social housing production targets 100,000+ new HLM units per year. Territorial programmes affect thousands of communes.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Cohésion des territoires; ANAH annual report; Cour des comptes MaPrimeRénov' audit 2024.

mission: Agriculture, alimentation, forêt et affaires rurales
name: Compétitivité et durabilité de l'agriculture, de l'agroalimentaire et de la forêt
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; agricultural support mechanisms governed by Code rural, loi d'orientation agricole 2006, loi Egalim 2018/2021. Complements EU CAP funding)
description: Funds national agricultural support measures complementing the EU Common Agricultural Policy: CUMA investment grants, installation aid for young farmers, plant/animal health services, SAFER operations, forestry policy (ONF), and agricultural crisis response.
beneficiaries: Approximately 390,000 farms in France, with particular focus on young farmer installation (about 14,000 annual installations) and small/medium farms in transition. ONF manages 11 million hectares of public forests.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission AAFAR; Agreste (SSP) statistics; Cour des comptes agricultural policy reports.

name: Service public de l'énergie
created: 2015 (programme created following the restructuring of energy policy financing; CSPE mechanism reformed by loi de transition énergétique n°2015-992 du 17 août 2015)
description: Funds public service obligations in the energy sector: support for renewable energy producers (obligation d'achat), electricity price equalisation for overseas territories (péréquation tarifaire), energy poverty measures (chèque énergie), and Mediateur de l'énergie.
beneficiaries: Chèque énergie reaches approximately 5.6 million low-income households. Overseas territories (2.2 million inhabitants) benefit from price equalisation. Thousands of renewable energy producers receive feed-in tariffs or complements de rémunération.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Écologie; CRE (Commission de régulation de l'énergie) annual assessment of CSPE charges; Cour des comptes energy reports.

name: Concours financiers aux communes et groupements de communes
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; DGF created by loi n°79-15 du 3 janvier 1979, reformed multiple times, most recently by loi de finances 2024. Includes DGF, DETR, DSIL, FCTVA)
description: Channels State grants to communes and intercommunal bodies (EPCI): the Dotation globale de fonctionnement (DGF), Dotation d'équipement des territoires ruraux (DETR), Dotation de soutien à l'investissement local (DSIL), and related equalisation grants.
beneficiaries: Approximately 35,000 communes and 1,250+ EPCIs receive DGF. DETR benefits about 33,000 eligible rural communes. DSIL supports investment in communes of all sizes. Total envelope approximately 28 Md€ including FCTVA across the broader mission.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Relations avec les collectivités territoriales; DGCL annual report on local finances; CFL (Comité des finances locales) publications.

name: Vie étudiante
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; CROUS network established by loi n°55-425 du 16 avril 1955, reformed by ordonnance 2018. Bourses sur critères sociaux system codified in Code de l'éducation)
description: Funds student grants (bourses sur critères sociaux), university student housing (CROUS residences), student restaurants (RU at subsidised prices), and student health services. Managed through the CNOUS/CROUS network.
beneficiaries: Approximately 800,000 students receiving bourses sur critères sociaux, 175,000 CROUS housing places, and student restaurants serving approximately 300,000 meals daily across 700+ points of service.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - MIRES programme 231; CNOUS annual report; OVE (Observatoire de la vie étudiante) surveys.

mission: Agriculture, alimentation, forêt et affaires rurales
name: Sécurité et qualité sanitaires de l'alimentation
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; food safety framework from EU regulations transposed into Code rural. DGAL missions, ANSES created 2010 merging AFSSA+AFSSET)
description: Funds official food safety controls (DGAL inspections), animal health programmes (vaccination campaigns, epizootic response including avian influenza), phytosanitary inspections, and the food safety agency ANSES's contribution.
beneficiaries: Protects the entire French population (68 million) through food safety. Directly involves approximately 400,000 food establishments under inspection, livestock farms for animal health surveillance, and agricultural producers subject to phytosanitary controls.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission AAFAR; DGAL annual plan; ANSES annual report; Cour des comptes food safety audits.

mission: Avances à l'audiovisuel public (compte de concours financiers, replaced by budget général from 2023)
name: France Télévisions
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; France Télévisions created by loi n°2000-719 du 1er août 2000 merging France 2, France 3, La Cinquième. Redevance abolished in 2022, replaced by TVA fraction then budget général)
description: Funds the public television group France Télévisions (France 2, France 3, France 5, franceinfo, Outre-mer la 1ère, france.tv). Since 2023 suppression of the redevance, funded directly from the State budget to guarantee public service broadcasting independence.
beneficiaries: France Télévisions reaches approximately 50 million weekly viewers across its channels. Employs about 9,300 staff. Serves the entire French-speaking population including overseas territories through regional programming.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - budget audiovisuel public; loi n°2022-1157 suppressing redevance; CSA/Arcom reports; Cour des comptes France Télévisions audit.

name: Patrimoines
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; heritage protection from loi monuments historiques 1913, Code du patrimoine consolidated 2004, reinforced by loi LCAP 2016)
description: Funds the preservation, restoration, and promotion of France's cultural heritage: historic monuments (45,000+ protected sites), national museums (musées nationaux including Louvre, Orsay, Versailles), archives nationales, and archaeological research (INRAP).
beneficiaries: France's 45,000+ monuments historiques (14,000 classés, 31,000 inscrits), approximately 1,200 musées de France, and millions of annual visitors. The Louvre alone receives about 9 million visitors yearly. Indirectly benefits all heritage property owners receiving restoration grants.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Culture; Ministère de la Culture key figures; Cour des comptes heritage audit; Fondation du patrimoine.

name: Création
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; arts support policies from André Malraux's Ministry of Culture 1959, loi relative à la liberté de la création 2016)
description: Funds support for artistic creation across performing arts (theatre, dance, music, circus, street arts), visual arts, and cultural venues: national theatres, opera houses, orchestras, contemporary art centres (FRAC), and artist residency programmes.
beneficiaries: Supports approximately 350 labellised cultural institutions (CDN, Scènes nationales, SMAC, etc.), thousands of subsidised artistic companies and individual artists, and the national opera and theatre companies (Opéra de Paris, Comédie-Française, etc.).
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Culture; DGCA (Direction générale de la création artistique) data; Cour des comptes spectacle vivant reports.

name: Infrastructures et services de transports
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; transport infrastructure policy governed by LOTI 1982, loi d'orientation des mobilités LOM 2019. AFIT France created 2019)
description: Funds maintenance and development of State transport infrastructure: national road network (12,000 km), waterways (Voies navigables de France), port infrastructure, rail network contributions (alongside SNCF Réseau), and intermodal transport services.
beneficiaries: Serves the entire French population through transport networks. Specifically: users of 12,000 km of national roads, 6,700 km of navigable waterways, 11 major maritime ports (grands ports maritimes), and railway infrastructure maintenance benefiting 4.7 million daily rail passengers.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Écologie; AFIT France (ex-AFITF) budget; Cour des comptes transport infrastructure reports; COI (Conseil d'orientation des infrastructures).

name: Sport
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; sports policy governed by Code du sport. Agence nationale du Sport (ANS) created by loi n°2019-812. Budget significantly increased for Paris 2024 Olympics)
description: Funds the development of sport in France: high-performance sport (INSEP, preparation for Olympic/Paralympic games), grassroots sport development through the Agence nationale du Sport, anti-doping agency (AFLD), and major sporting events hosting.
beneficiaries: Approximately 16 million licensed athletes in 360,000 sports clubs across France. High-performance programme supports about 7,000 athletes on the ministerial lists. Paris 2024 preparations involved significant infrastructure investment.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Sport, jeunesse et vie associative; ANS annual report; INJEP statistics; SOLIDEO budget.

name: Jeunesse et vie associative
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; youth and community policy from loi associations 1901. Service civique created by loi n°2010-241 du 10 mars 2010. SNU announced 2019)
description: Funds youth engagement programmes, primarily the Service civique (voluntary civic service for 16-25 year-olds), the Service national universel (SNU), FDVA grants to associations, youth information networks, and support for community organisations (vie associative).
beneficiaries: Service civique engages approximately 150,000 young volunteers annually. SNU has involved several tens of thousands of participants since its experimental launch. FDVA supports thousands of local associations. Overall approximately 1.3 million associations in France benefit from the policy framework.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Sport, jeunesse et vie associative; Agence du service civique annual report; INJEP études.

name: Prévention des risques
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; risk prevention governed by Code de l'environnement, loi Bachelot 2003 on technological risks, Grenelle laws 2009-2010, ADEME missions)
description: Funds prevention and management of technological, natural, and health-environmental risks: ICPE (classified installations) inspections, flood prevention plans (PAPI), contaminated site remediation, ADEME contributions, and air quality monitoring (AASQA networks).
beneficiaries: Protects the entire population from environmental risks. Specifically: 500,000+ ICPE installations subject to inspection, 17 million residents in flood-prone areas, populations near Seveso sites (about 1,300 in France), and communities affected by former mining/industrial contamination.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Écologie; DGPR activity report; ADEME annual report; Cour des comptes ICPE inspection audit.

name: Énergie, climat et après-mines
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; energy/climate policy from loi transition énergétique 2015, loi énergie-climat 2019, loi climat et résilience 2021. Après-mines from Code minier)
description: Funds energy transition measures (hydrogen strategy, decarbonisation of industry), climate change adaptation, and post-mining obligations (former coal basin environmental remediation in Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Lorraine, etc. via BRGM/DREAL).
beneficiaries: Post-mining measures serve populations in former coal and mining basins (several hundred thousand residents). Energy transition measures benefit industrial sectors and territorial climate plans. Hydrogen strategy supports emerging industrial ecosystem.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Écologie; SGPE (Secrétariat général à la planification écologique); Stratégie nationale bas-carbone (SNBC); Cour des comptes energy transition reports.

name: Sécurité civile
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; civil protection governed by loi de modernisation de la sécurité civile n°2004-811 du 13 août 2004. DGSCGC under Ministry of Interior)
description: Funds national civil protection resources: aerial firefighting fleet (Canadair, Dash), bomb disposal units (déminage), FORMISC military emergency units, COGIC crisis coordination centre, and support to departmental fire services (SDIS).
beneficiaries: Protects the entire French population (68 million). The aerial fleet fights about 3,000-4,000 wildfires annually. Approximately 250,000 sapeurs-pompiers (40,000 professional, 195,000 volunteer, 15,000 military in Paris/Marseille) form the national response system.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Sécurités; DGSCGC annual report; Cour des comptes sécurité civile audit; SDIS national statistics.

name: Immigration et asile
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; asylum governed by Code de l'entrée et du séjour des étrangers, OFPRA created 1952, OFII missions, CNDA. Recent reform: loi n°2024-42 du 26 janvier 2024)
description: Funds the asylum system (OFPRA processing, CNDA appeals court, CMA reception centres), immigration enforcement (CRA detention centres, removal operations), integration programmes (CIR), and the OFII agency operations.
beneficiaries: OFPRA processes approximately 140,000-160,000 asylum applications annually. National reception system (DNA) provides about 110,000 places in CADA/HUDA/CPH centres. CIR integration programme serves about 110,000 newly arrived legal immigrants per year.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Immigration, asile et intégration; OFPRA annual report; OFII activity report; Cour des comptes immigration audits.

name: Enseignement technique agricole
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; agricultural education governed by Code rural et de la pêche maritime, loi d'avenir pour l'agriculture 2014. Under joint authority of Ministry of Agriculture and Education)
description: Funds public and private agricultural secondary and technical education (lycées agricoles, MFR), covering teacher salaries, operating costs, and farm/laboratory infrastructure in agricultural educational establishments.
beneficiaries: Approximately 190,000 students (about 60% in private establishments under contract, 40% in public lycées agricoles), with approximately 15,000 teachers. Includes 800+ establishments offering CAP to BTS-level qualifications in agriculture, agro-food, and environment.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Enseignement scolaire; DGER (Direction générale de l'enseignement et de la recherche) statistics; Cour des comptes agricultural education audit.

name: Protection judiciaire de la jeunesse
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; juvenile justice governed by ordonnance n°45-174 du 2 février 1945, replaced by Code de la justice pénale des mineurs entered into force 30 September 2021)
description: Funds the judicial protection of minors: educational measures ordered by juvenile judges, youth detention centres (CEF), juvenile prisons (EPM), open-environment educational programmes (milieu ouvert), and the sector associatif habilité providing care services.
beneficiaries: Approximately 150,000 minors followed by PJJ services annually (both penal and civil measures), with about 1,200 places in CEF and 800 in EPM/quartiers mineurs. The DPJJ employs about 9,000 staff across 220+ units.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Justice; DPJJ annual report; Code de la justice pénale des mineurs; CGLPL inspections.

name: Politique de la ville
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; urban policy from loi Borloo n°2003-710, current framework from loi Lamy n°2014-173 du 21 février 2014 defining QPV - quartiers prioritaires)
description: Funds urban renewal and social cohesion programmes in designated priority neighbourhoods (QPV): ANRU urban renovation projects (NPNRU), cité éducative programmes, employment insertion, social mediation, and associations working in disadvantaged urban areas.
beneficiaries: Approximately 5.4 million residents living in 1,514 QPV (quartiers prioritaires de la politique de la ville). NPNRU covers 450 neighbourhoods with major urban renewal projects (estimated 27 Md€ total investment over 10+ years including leveraged funds).
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Cohésion des territoires; ONPV (Observatoire national de la politique de la ville) annual report; ANRU financial reports; Cour des comptes QPV audit.

name: Coordination du travail gouvernemental
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; services of the Prime Minister, SGDSN created 2009 replacing SGDN, ANSSI created 2009, DILA, CNIL, Défenseur des droits)
description: Funds the Prime Minister's coordination services: SGDSN (defence/national security secretariat), ANSSI (cybersecurity agency), strategic intelligence coordination, government communication (SIG), independent administrative authorities (CNIL, CADA), and interministerial coordination bodies.
beneficiaries: Not applicable as individual beneficiaries. ANSSI protects approximately 700 OIV (opérateurs d'importance vitale) and critical infrastructure. CNIL handles approximately 16,000 complaints annually. Services benefit the entire government apparatus and indirectly the whole population.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Direction de l'action du Gouvernement; ANSSI annual report; SGDSN activity report; CNIL annual report.

name: Conseil d'État et autres juridictions administratives
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; Conseil d'État traces to Constitution of 22 frimaire an VIII (1799), modern administrative justice from loi du 31 décembre 1987 creating CAA. CNDA added 2009)
description: Funds the administrative justice system: Conseil d'État (supreme administrative court and government legal advisor), 42 tribunaux administratifs, 9 cours administratives d'appel, and the Cour nationale du droit d'asile (CNDA) which handles asylum appeals.
beneficiaries: Administrative courts handle approximately 250,000 cases annually (including about 70,000 CNDA asylum appeals). Employs about 4,200 staff including approximately 1,300 magistrats administratifs. Serves all persons contesting administrative decisions.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Conseil et contrôle de l'État; Conseil d'État annual report; CNDA activity statistics.

name: Financement des investissements stratégiques
created: 2021 (created by loi n°2021-1900 du 30 décembre 2021, LFI 2022; implements President Macron's France 2030 investment plan announced October 2021, succeeding PIA 1-3-4)
description: Funds France 2030 strategic investments (54 Md€ total envelope): decarbonised hydrogen, electric vehicles/batteries, nuclear innovation (SMR), biotechnologies, space, semiconductors, quantum computing, key minerals, and cultural/creative industries. Managed by the SGPI.
beneficiaries: Supports thousands of businesses, startups, and research laboratories across strategic sectors. Key targets: 8 gigafactories for batteries, 2 SMR nuclear reactors, 20 biomedical innovations, and a French semiconductor ecosystem. No single individual beneficiary count.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Investir pour la France de 2030; SGPI (Secrétariat général pour l'investissement) dashboard; Cour des comptes PIA/France 2030 evaluation.

name: Emploi outre-mer
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; overseas employment support governed by loi de programme pour l'outre-mer n°2003-660 du 21 juillet 2003 (Girardin), LODEOM n°2009-594 du 27 mai 2009)
description: Funds employment support measures specific to overseas territories: employer social contribution exemptions (exonérations Lodeom), service militaire adapté (SMA) vocational training for youth, and insertion programmes tailored to DOM-TOM labour markets.
beneficiaries: Approximately 100,000+ employers in overseas territories benefit from Lodeom contribution exemptions. SMA trains about 6,000 young volunteers annually (aged 18-25) across 8 overseas regiments. Total overseas population: 2.2 million.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Outre-mer; DARES overseas employment statistics; SMA annual report; IEDOM/IEOM economic bulletins.

name: Protection maladie
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; AME (Aide médicale d'État) created by loi CMU n°99-1140 du 27 décembre 1999, replacing earlier aide médicale gratuite. Reformed by loi immigration 2024)
description: Funds the Aide médicale d'État (AME), which provides healthcare coverage to undocumented immigrants residing in France for more than 3 months and meeting income criteria. Also covers soins urgents for those not eligible for AME and healthcare for other specific populations.
beneficiaries: Approximately 400,000 AME beneficiaries (as of end 2023, rising trend). Soins urgents cover additional undocumented persons not meeting AME criteria. The programme has been politically contentious with periodic reform debates.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Santé; CNAM AME statistics; IGAS evaluation reports; Cour des comptes AME audit 2019.

name: Solidarité à l'égard des pays en développement
created: 2006 (LOLF programme; development cooperation policy framed by loi n°2021-1031 du 4 août 2021. MEAE cooperation instruments, Expertise France, Volontariat)
description: Funds bilateral development cooperation managed by the Ministry of Europe and Foreign Affairs (MEAE): grants to developing countries for governance, health, education, and food security; contributions to multilateral organisations (Global Fund, GAVI, GPE); crisis humanitarian aid; Expertise France; and French development volunteers.
beneficiaries: Indirect beneficiaries across 19 priority partner countries (primarily Sahel, West Africa, Great Lakes). Key multilateral contributions: Global Fund against AIDS/TB/Malaria (serving millions), GAVI vaccination alliance, Global Partnership for Education. French development volunteers number about 2,000.
source_notes: PLF 2024 - Mission Aide publique au développement; MEAE DGM cooperation reports; OECD DAC France statistics; loi de programmation développement solidaire 2021.

name: Contrôle de la circulation et du stationnement routiers
description: Mission managing automated speed enforcement, traffic fine processing, and redistribution of fine revenue to road safety and local authorities.
beneficiaries: Road users, local authorities, road safety agencies

name: Gestion du patrimoine immobilier de l'État
description: Mission managing the state's real estate portfolio including maintenance, acquisition, disposal, and optimization of government buildings.
beneficiaries: State agencies, government employees, France Domaine

name: Crédits non répartis
description: Mission holding unallocated reserve appropriations for unforeseen expenditures and public salary provisions, distributed to other missions as needed.
beneficiaries: All government ministries (as contingency reserve)

name: Engagements financiers de l'État
description: Mission covering sovereign debt service, state guarantees called upon, and debt amortization including COVID-related borrowing.
beneficiaries: Bondholders, state creditors, guarantee beneficiaries

name: Prêts et avances à divers services de l'État
description: Mission providing treasury loans and advances to various state services, agencies, and public establishments with repayment obligations.
beneficiaries: State agencies, public establishments, budget annexes

name: Participations financières de l'État
description: Mission managing the state's shareholder portfolio, capital operations in public enterprises, and strategic equity investments.
beneficiaries: State-owned enterprises (EDF, SNCF, etc.), Agence des participations de l'État

name: Économie
description: Mission funding economic development programs, business support, competition oversight, consumer protection, and industrial policy implementation.
beneficiaries: SMEs, entrepreneurs, consumers, INSEE, DGCCRF

name: Aide publique au développement
description: Mission channeling France's official development assistance through bilateral and multilateral cooperation, humanitarian aid, and development finance.
beneficiaries: Developing countries, AFD, international organizations, NGOs

name: Prêts à des États étrangers
description: Mission providing concessional and treasury loans to foreign governments, often tied to development objectives or trade facilitation.
beneficiaries: Foreign sovereign governments, primarily in Africa and emerging economies

name: Prêts et avances à des particuliers ou à des organismes privés
description: Mission providing state loans and advances to individuals and private entities for specific policy purposes such as housing or social needs.
beneficiaries: Individuals, private organizations, social economy actors

name: Enseignement scolaire
description: Mission funding primary and secondary education including teacher salaries, school operations, private education contracts, and agricultural education.
beneficiaries: Students (K-12), teachers, school support staff, private schools under contract

name: Défense
description: Mission financing military capabilities, equipment procurement, operational readiness, military personnel, and defense research.
beneficiaries: Armed forces personnel, defense industry, DGA, military intelligence

name: Sécurités
description: Mission funding the national police, gendarmerie, civil security, and road safety programs to ensure internal security across French territory.
beneficiaries: Police nationale, Gendarmerie nationale, firefighters, citizens

name: Administration générale et territoriale de l'État
description: Mission funding prefectures, sub-prefectures, and territorial state administration ensuring government presence and public order across departments.
beneficiaries: Prefectures, sub-prefectures, local populations, elected officials

name: Immigration, asile et intégration
description: Mission managing asylum procedures, immigration enforcement, reception of asylum seekers, and integration programs for legal immigrants.
beneficiaries: Asylum seekers, refugees, legal immigrants, OFPRA, OFII

name: Solidarité, insertion et égalité des chances
description: Mission funding social inclusion programs, disability allowances (AAH), child protection, gender equality policies, and anti-poverty measures.
beneficiaries: Disabled persons, low-income households, women, children in protection, elderly

name: Recherche et enseignement supérieur
description: Mission funding university operations, student grants, public research bodies (CNRS, INSERM, etc.), and innovation ecosystems.
beneficiaries: Universities, researchers, students, CNRS, INSERM, INRAE, CEA

name: Cohésion des territoires
description: Mission funding housing aid, urban renewal, territorial planning, and digital connectivity to reduce geographic inequalities.
beneficiaries: Social housing tenants, disadvantaged urban areas, rural communities, ANRU

name: Travail, emploi et administration des ministères sociaux
description: Mission funding employment policy, vocational training subsidies, Pôle emploi/France Travail, apprenticeship incentives, and social ministry operations.
beneficiaries: Job seekers, apprentices, employers, France Travail, DREETS

name: Écologie, développement et mobilité durables
description: Mission financing sustainable transport infrastructure, water policy, biodiversity, energy transition, risk prevention, and meteorological services.
beneficiaries: Transport users, environmental agencies, ADEME, Météo-France, waterway operators

name: Relations avec les collectivités territoriales
description: Mission managing financial grants (DGE, DSIL, DETR) and equalization transfers from the state to local authorities.
beneficiaries: Municipalities, departments, regions, intercommunal bodies

name: Contrôle et exploitation aériens
description: Mission funding air navigation services, air traffic control operations, and aviation safety oversight via the DGAC budget annexe.
beneficiaries: Airlines, air passengers, DGAC, air traffic controllers

name: Audiovisuel public
description: Mission funding public broadcasting entities including France Télévisions, Radio France, France Médias Monde, Arte France, and INA.
beneficiaries: France Télévisions, Radio France, Arte, viewers and listeners

name: Médias, livre et industries culturelles
description: Mission supporting press pluralism, book industry, music, cinema, and cultural industries through subsidies and regulatory frameworks.
beneficiaries: Press outlets, publishers, booksellers, libraries, cultural industry professionals

name: Investir pour la France de 2030
description: Mission channeling the France 2030 investment plan funds into strategic sectors: nuclear, hydrogen, semiconductors, health, space, and deep tech.
beneficiaries: Innovative startups, research labs, industrial champions, BPI France

name: Direction de l'action du Gouvernement
description: Mission funding interministerial coordination bodies, SGDSN, DINUM, government communication, and strategic advisory councils under the PM.
beneficiaries: Prime Minister's office, SGDSN, DINUM, interministerial bodies

name: Conseil et contrôle de l'État
description: Mission funding the Conseil d'État, Cour des comptes, CESE, and other consultative/oversight bodies ensuring administrative and financial control.
beneficiaries: Conseil d'État, Cour des comptes, CESE, administrative courts

name: Action extérieure de l'État
description: Mission funding diplomatic network operations, consular services, cultural diplomacy (Instituts français, Alliance française), and French schools abroad.
beneficiaries: French embassies, consulates, French nationals abroad, AEFE schools

name: Agriculture, alimentation, forêt et affaires rurales
description: Mission funding agricultural subsidies, food safety, forestry management (ONF), rural development, and veterinary/phytosanitary services.
beneficiaries: Farmers, agri-food businesses, ONF, rural communities, FranceAgriMer

name: Outre-mer
description: Mission funding specific programs for overseas territories including economic development, housing, education, and territorial continuity.
beneficiaries: Overseas territory residents and local governments (Guadeloupe, Martinique, Réunion, Guyane, Mayotte, etc.)

name: Santé
description: Mission funding public health prevention programs, health security agencies, healthcare access initiatives, and bioethics-related activities.
beneficiaries: Patients, health agencies (Santé publique France, ANSM, INCa), healthcare workers

name: Sport, jeunesse et vie associative
description: Mission funding elite and grassroots sports, youth civic service, associative sector support, and major sporting event preparation.
beneficiaries: Athletes, sports federations, youth organizations, nonprofits, civic service volunteers

name: Gestion fiscale et financière de l'État et du secteur public local
description: Programme funding DGFiP operations: tax assessment and collection, public accounting, land registry, and financial management for local authorities.
beneficiaries: DGFiP agents, taxpayers, local authorities, state accountants

name: Pensions civiles et militaires de retraite et allocations temporaires d'invalidité
description: Programme paying retirement pensions to former civil servants and military personnel, plus temporary disability allowances for active agents.
beneficiaries: Retired civil servants, retired military, disabled active civil servants

name: Charge de la dette et trésorerie de l'État (crédits évaluatifs)
description: Programme covering interest payments on government bonds (OAT, BTF), debt swaps, and state cash management costs.
beneficiaries: Bondholders, financial markets, Agence France Trésor

name: Appels en garantie de l'État (crédits évaluatifs)
description: Programme covering payments triggered when state-guaranteed loans or commitments default, including export guarantees and development bank guarantees.
beneficiaries: Guarantee beneficiaries (exporters, banks, international institutions)

name: Opérations en capital intéressant les participations financières de l'État
description: Programme financing capital injections, equity acquisitions, and divestiture operations in state-owned enterprises and strategic companies.
beneficiaries: State-owned enterprises, APE, strategic industrial companies

name: Désendettement de l'État et d'établissements publics de l'État
description: Programme using privatization and divestiture proceeds to reduce public debt of the state and its public establishments.
beneficiaries: State budget, public establishments, future taxpayers

name: Pensions militaires d'invalidité et des victimes de guerre et autres pensions
description: Programme paying disability pensions to war-injured military personnel, war victims, and their dependents, plus miscellaneous legacy pensions.
beneficiaries: War invalids, war widows/orphans, veterans, victims of terrorism

name: Régimes de retraite et de sécurité sociale des marins, des mines, de la SEITA et divers
description: Programme subsidizing legacy pension and social security schemes for miners, former SEITA tobacco workers, and merchant navy personnel.
beneficiaries: Retired miners, former SEITA employees, merchant navy retirees

name: Dépenses accidentelles et imprévisibles
description: Contingency reserve for genuinely unforeseen expenditures such as natural disasters, crises, or emergency interventions not budgeted elsewhere.
beneficiaries: Any ministry or agency facing an unforeseen emergency

name: Compétitivité
description: Competitiveness component of the Plan de relance funding business digitalization, industrial modernization, R&D, and skills upgrading.
beneficiaries: SMEs, industrial firms, research labs, training organizations, BPI France

name: Acces au droit et a la justice
description: Ensures access to legal aid and justice for all citizens, including legal aid (aide juridictionnelle), mediation, and victim support services.
beneficiaries: Citizens seeking legal assistance, low-income litigants, crime victims

name: Acces et retour a l'emploi
mission: Travail, emploi et administration des ministeres sociaux
description: Supports access to employment and return-to-work programmes, including subsidized contracts and integration pathways for jobseekers.
beneficiaries: Jobseekers, long-term unemployed, youth entering labor market

name: Accompagnement des mutations economiques et developpement de l'emploi
mission: Travail, emploi et administration des ministeres sociaux
description: Supports economic transitions and employment development through vocational training, apprenticeship incentives, and skills adaptation programmes.
beneficiaries: Workers in economic transition, apprentices, employers

name: Integration et acces a la nationalite francaise
description: Finances integration programmes for immigrants and manages the naturalization process, including language courses and civic training via OFII.
beneficiaries: Immigrants, refugees, naturalization applicants

name: Actions en faveur des familles vulnerables
mission: Solidarite, insertion et egalite des chances
description: Supported vulnerable families through child protection measures and parenting support. Merged into programme 304.
beneficiaries: Vulnerable families, children in danger

name: Administration penitentiaire
description: Manages the prison system including detention facilities, prison staff, inmate rehabilitation, probation services, and prison construction programmes.
beneficiaries: Prison administration, inmates, probation officers

name: Aide a l'acces au logement
description: Finances housing allowances (APL, ALS, ALF) to help low-income households afford decent housing. Funds the FNAL (Fonds national d'aide au logement).
beneficiaries: Low-income tenants, students, families receiving housing benefits

name: Aide economique et financiere au developpement
description: Provides economic and financial development aid through bilateral loans, debt relief, and contributions to multilateral development institutions (World Bank, AfDB, ADB).
beneficiaries: Developing countries, multilateral development banks

name: Amelioration de la qualite de l'emploi et des relations du travail
mission: Travail, emploi et administration des ministeres sociaux
description: Funds labour inspection, occupational health improvement, social dialogue promotion, and enforcement of labour law.
beneficiaries: Workers, employers, trade unions, labour inspectors

name: Impulsion et coordination de la politique d'amenagement du territoire
description: Coordinates territorial planning policy through the ANCT (Agence nationale de la cohesion des territoires), supporting rural revitalization and balanced regional development.
beneficiaries: Rural communities, underdeveloped territories, local authorities, ANCT

name: Paysages, eau et biodiversite
description: Manages landscape protection, water policy, and biodiversity conservation including Natura 2000 sites, national parks, and water agency coordination.
beneficiaries: OFB, national parks, water agencies, environmental NGOs

name: Appels en garantie de l'Etat (credits evaluatifs)
description: Covers calls on State guarantees when guaranteed entities default, including export credit guarantees (BPI) and financial sector backstops. Credits are evaluative (non-limitative).
beneficiaries: Entities benefiting from State guarantees

name: Charge de la dette et tresorerie de l'Etat (credits evaluatifs)
description: Covers interest payments on government debt (OAT bonds) and treasury management costs, managed by Agence France Tresor. The largest single programme by expenditure.
beneficiaries: Agence France Tresor, bondholders

name: Concours financiers aux collectivites territoriales et a leurs groupements
description: Provides financial transfers to local authorities (communes, departments, regions) including the DGF (dotation globale de fonctionnement), DETR, and DSIL investment grants.
beneficiaries: Communes, departments, regions, intercommunal groupings

name: Concours specifiques et administration
description: Manages specific financial support to local authorities facing exceptional circumstances (natural disasters, fiscal difficulties) and programme administration costs.
beneficiaries: Local authorities in financial distress, disaster-affected communes

name: Conduite et soutien des politiques sanitaires et sociales
mission: Solidarite, insertion et egalite des chances
description: Cross-cutting support for health/social ministries. Restructured into programme 155.
beneficiaries: Social ministry staff

name: Conseil economique, social et environnemental
description: Funds the CESE, the constitutional consultative assembly representing civil society (unions, employers, associations) that advises on economic, social, and environmental policy.
beneficiaries: CESE members, civil society representatives

name: Creation
description: Supports artistic creation across performing arts (theatre, dance, music), visual arts, and contemporary creation through subsidies to cultural institutions and artists.
beneficiaries: Artists, theatres, opera houses, art centers, contemporary art institutions

name: Developpement des entreprises et regulations
description: Supports business development, competition regulation (DGCCRF), consumer protection, industrial policy, and the postal service universal obligation (La Poste).
beneficiaries: Businesses, consumers, DGCCRF, La Poste

name: Urbanisme, territoires et amelioration de l'habitat
description: Manages urban planning policy, housing renovation (ANAH), construction regulation, social housing oversight, and the Action Coeur de Ville programme.
beneficiaries: Social housing tenants, homeowners renovating, ANAH, local urban planners

name: Egalite entre les femmes et les hommes
mission: Solidarite, insertion et egalite des chances
description: Promotes gender equality through anti-violence programmes (shelters, hotline 3919), professional equality measures, and support for women's rights associations.
beneficiaries: Women, victims of domestic violence, gender equality organizations

name: Enseignement prive du premier et du second degres
description: Funds State-contracted private primary and secondary education, covering teacher salaries and forfait communal under the Debarre law (1959).
beneficiaries: Students in private schools under contract, private school teachers

name: Enseignement scolaire public du premier degre
description: Finances public primary education (ecoles maternelles and elementaires), including teacher salaries, pedagogical support, and inclusive education.
beneficiaries: Public primary school students (ages 3-11), primary school teachers

name: Enseignement scolaire public du second degre
description: Finances public secondary education (colleges and lycees), covering teacher salaries, curriculum delivery, and school operations. The largest programme by headcount.
beneficiaries: Public secondary school students (ages 11-18), secondary teachers

name: Enseignement superieur et recherche agricoles
description: Funds higher education and research in agriculture, veterinary science, and food science through AgroParisTech, veterinary schools, and INRAE partnerships.
beneficiaries: Agricultural engineering schools, veterinary schools, agronomy students

name: Environnement et prospective de la politique de defense
description: Gathers intelligence, strategic analysis, and defense foresight under DGRIS, including military intelligence (DRM, DRSD), defense R&D, and international relations of the Ministry of Armed Forces.
beneficiaries: DGRIS, DRM, DRSD, defense research laboratories, IRSEM

name: Epargne
description: Covers State obligations related to regulated savings products, particularly interest bonuses for Livret A, LDD, LEP, and PEL savings schemes.
beneficiaries: Holders of regulated savings accounts (Livret A, LDD, LEP)

name: Equipement des forces
description: Funds acquisition, development, and maintenance of major military equipment (Rafale, FREMM frigates, Scorpion, Barracuda submarines, missiles) under the DGA procurement agency.
beneficiaries: DGA, defense industry (Dassault, Naval Group, MBDA, Thales), armed forces

name: Competitivite et durabilite de l'agriculture, de l'agroalimentaire et de la foret
mission: Agriculture, alimentation, foret et affaires rurales
description: Supports agricultural competitiveness and sustainability through CAP co-financing, agricultural modernization, forestry policy (ONF), and agri-food sector investments.
beneficiaries: Farmers, agri-food businesses, ONF, forestry sector, agricultural cooperatives

name: Formations superieures et recherche universitaire
description: Funds universities and higher education institutions for teaching and research. The largest programme in the MIRES mission (EUR 15.3 billion).
beneficiaries: Universities, grandes ecoles, higher education students, university researchers

name: Francais a l'etranger et affaires consulaires
description: Manages consular services for French nationals abroad (2.5 million registered) including civil status, visa processing, emergency assistance, and AEFE school network oversight.
beneficiaries: French nationals abroad, foreign visa applicants, consular staff

name: Soutien des ministeres sociaux
mission: Travail, emploi et administration des ministeres sociaux
description: Provides cross-cutting administrative support (HR, IT, logistics) for the ministries of labour, health, and social affairs, including DREETS regional services.
beneficiaries: Social ministries staff, DREETS regional directorates

name: Gestion fiscale et financiere de l'Etat et du secteur public local
description: Funds the DGFiP (100,000+ agents) for tax collection, public accounting, land registry (cadastre), financial management of local authorities, and property valuation.
beneficiaries: DGFiP, taxpayers, local authorities

name: Handicap et dependance
mission: Solidarite, insertion et egalite des chances
description: Finances the AAH disability allowance (1.2 million beneficiaries), ESAT sheltered workshops, and support for disabled persons' inclusion in employment and society.
beneficiaries: Disabled persons, AAH recipients, MDPH, ESAT workshops

name: Indemnisation des victimes des persecutions antisemites et des actes de barbarie pendant la seconde guerre mondiale
mission: Anciens combattants, memoire et liens avec la Nation
description: Provides compensation to victims of anti-Semitic persecution and acts of barbarity during World War II, including orphans and descendants under 1999 and 2004 decrees.
beneficiaries: WWII persecution victims and their orphans/descendants

name: Expertise, information geographique et meteorologie
description: Funds Meteo-France (national weather service), IGN (geographic/mapping institute), and CEREMA (center for expertise on risks, environment, mobility, and planning).
beneficiaries: Meteo-France, IGN, CEREMA, general public

name: Securite civile
description: Manages civil protection including firefighting aircraft fleet (Canadairs, Dash), bomb disposal (DGSCGC), NRBC defense, and crisis management coordination with departmental fire services.
beneficiaries: DGSCGC, SDIS fire services, populations at risk from disasters

name: Interventions territoriales de l'Etat
description: Finances specific large-scale interministerial territorial projects (PEI) requiring rapid, flexible budgetary management in designated territories (Corsica, etc.).
beneficiaries: Targeted territories (Corsica, Seine-Saint-Denis, etc.)

name: Cour des comptes et autres juridictions financieres
description: Funds the Cour des comptes (national audit office) and regional/territorial audit chambers (CRC/CTC) for auditing public finances and evaluating public policies.
beneficiaries: Cour des comptes, CRC, Haut Conseil des finances publiques

name: Conseil d'Etat et autres juridictions administratives
description: Funds the Conseil d'Etat (supreme administrative court), 8 administrative courts of appeal, 42 administrative tribunals, and the CNDA (asylum appeal court).
beneficiaries: Conseil d'Etat, administrative courts, litigants in public law matters

name: Reconnaissance et reparation en faveur du monde combattant, memoire et liens avec la Nation
mission: Anciens combattants, memoire et liens avec la Nation
description: Provides pensions and benefits for veterans, maintains memorial sites, promotes remembrance through ONACVG, and strengthens defense-society links (JDC).
beneficiaries: Veterans, war widows, ONACVG, memorial sites

name: Meteorologie
description: Funded Meteo-France operations separately. Merged into programme 159.
beneficiaries: Meteo-France

name: Energie, climat et apres-mines
description: Finances energy transition: MaPrimeRenov housing renovation, vehicle conversion bonuses, energy cheques for 5.6 million low-income households, and post-mining territory management.
beneficiaries: Low-income households, homeowners (MaPrimeRenov), former mining regions

name: Hebergement, parcours vers le logement et insertion des personnes vulnerables
description: Funds emergency shelter (200,000+ places), transitional housing, social integration pathways for homeless and vulnerable persons, and the 115 emergency hotline.
beneficiaries: Homeless persons, asylum seekers in temporary housing, CHRS centers

name: Preparation et emploi des forces
description: Funds operational readiness, training, and deployment of the French armed forces (Army, Navy, Air & Space Force), including MCO equipment maintenance and military exercises.
beneficiaries: Armee de Terre, Marine nationale, Armee de l'Air et de l'Espace

name: Presse et medias
description: Supports press diversity and media pluralism through subsidies to press organizations, AFP (Agence France-Presse), postal distribution aid, and journalistic innovation.
beneficiaries: Press publishers, AFP, online media, journalists

name: Prevention des risques
description: Manages technological and natural risk prevention including the Barnier fund (flood/natural disaster), industrial safety regulation (ICPE/Seveso), and contaminated site remediation.
beneficiaries: Populations in risk zones, ADEME, industrial facilities, local authorities

name: Recherche dans les domaines de l'energie, du developpement et de la mobilite durables
description: Funds research in energy, sustainable development, and transport, primarily through CEA (nuclear/renewable energy), IFPEN (petroleum/new energies), and IRSN (nuclear safety).
beneficiaries: CEA, IFPEN, IRSN, energy research laboratories

name: Recherche duale (civile et militaire)
description: Funds dual-purpose civil-military research through CNES (space observation, telecommunications) and CEA (nuclear simulation), supporting technologies with both civilian and defense applications.
beneficiaries: CNES, CEA, dual-use technology researchers

name: Recherche et enseignement superieur en matiere economique et industrielle
description: Funds economic research and higher education under the economy ministry, including Institut Mines-Telecom, Ecole nationale de la statistique (ENSAE), and industrial innovation support.
beneficiaries: Institut Mines-Telecom, ENSAE, economic research institutes

name: Regimes de retraite des mines, de la SEITA, et divers
description: Funds the State subsidy to closed pension schemes for former miners (CANSSM), SEITA (tobacco) workers, and other legacy special retirement regimes in demographic decline.
beneficiaries: Retired miners, former SEITA employees, legacy pension scheme beneficiaries

name: Regimes de retraite et de securite sociale des marins
description: Subsidizes the ENIM (Etablissement National des Invalides de la Marine) pension and social security scheme for merchant and fishing seamen, one of France's oldest social schemes (1673).
beneficiaries: Retired seamen, ENIM beneficiaries

name: Regimes sociaux et de retraite des transports terrestres
description: Subsidizes the special pension regimes for SNCF rail workers (CPRPSNCF) and RATP Paris transit workers, covering structural deficits of these legacy systems.
beneficiaries: Retired SNCF workers, retired RATP workers

name: Remboursements et degrevements d'impots d'Etat (credits evaluatifs)
description: Covers tax refunds and relief for national taxes (income tax, corporate tax, VAT refunds) including excess payments, tax credits (CICE, CIR), and contentious refunds.
beneficiaries: Taxpayers entitled to refunds or tax relief

name: Remboursements et degrevements d'impots locaux (credits evaluatifs)
description: Covers refunds and relief for local taxes (property tax, CFE) borne by the State on behalf of local authorities, including compensation for abolished housing tax.
beneficiaries: Local taxpayers, local authorities

name: Prevention, securite sanitaire et offre de soins
description: Funds public health prevention, health security agencies (ANSES, Sante publique France), INCa (cancer), and healthcare provision coordination with regional health agencies (ARS).
beneficiaries: Sante publique France, ANSES, INCa, ARS, general population

name: Affaires maritimes, peche et aquaculture
description: Manages maritime affairs including maritime safety (CROSS), fisheries regulation (EU CFP implementation), aquaculture support, and French merchant marine registry.
beneficiaries: Fishermen, aquaculture operators, merchant marine, CROSS centers

name: Securite et qualite sanitaires de l'alimentation
mission: Agriculture, alimentation, foret et affaires rurales
description: Ensures food safety and phytosanitary controls through DGAL veterinary inspections, food chain monitoring, animal health surveillance, and plant protection.
beneficiaries: Consumers, food industry, farmers, DGAL veterinary services

name: Securite et education routieres
description: Manages road safety policy through the DSCR including driving license regulation, road safety education campaigns, and the national road safety observatory.
beneficiaries: Road users, driving schools, road safety associations

name: Solidarite a l'egard des pays en developpement
description: Implements bilateral development cooperation managed by MEAE including humanitarian aid, civil society support (NGOs), governance, health, and education programmes in partner countries.
beneficiaries: Developing countries, French development NGOs, partner states

name: Soutien de la politique de la defense
description: Provides cross-cutting support for defense: military HR management (DRHMD), infrastructure (SID), IT systems (DIRISI), financial management (DAF), and military justice.
beneficiaries: Ministry of Armed Forces, military personnel, defense infrastructure

name: Soutien de la politique de l'education nationale
description: Administrative support for national education: HR management, IT systems (SI), evaluation and statistics (DEPP), international cooperation, and ministry logistics for 1.1 million staff.
beneficiaries: Ministry of National Education staff, DEPP, school administration

name: Conduite et pilotage des politiques de l'agriculture
mission: Agriculture, alimentation, foret et affaires rurales
description: Administrative steering for agricultural policies: HR, IT, statistics (Agreste), legal affairs, and regional agricultural services (DRAAF/DAAF).
beneficiaries: Ministry of Agriculture staff, DRAAF regional directorates

name: Conduite et pilotage des politiques de l'interieur
mission: Administration generale et territoriale de l'Etat
description: Cross-cutting administrative support for interior ministry: HR, legal affairs, IT, real estate management, and internal audit services.
beneficiaries: Ministry of Interior staff and services

name: Conduite et pilotage des politiques de l'ecologie, du developpement et de la mobilite durables
description: Administrative support for ecology ministry: HR, legal services, IT systems, communication, and regional DREAL directorates coordination.
beneficiaries: Ministry of Ecological Transition staff, DREAL directorates

name: Conduite et pilotage des politiques economiques et financieres
description: Cross-cutting support for economic/financial ministries: Secretary General, HR, IT modernization, legal affairs, IGPDE training institute, and communication.
beneficiaries: Ministry of Economy and Finance staff, IGPDE

name: Statistiques et etudes economiques
description: Funds INSEE (5,000+ agents) for producing official statistics, economic studies, the national census, CPI inflation index, and national accounts.
beneficiaries: INSEE, policy makers, researchers, general public

name: Tourisme
description: Tourism policy through Atout France agency. Merged into programme 134.
beneficiaries: Atout France, tourism industry

name: Soutien aux politiques du ministere de la culture
description: Cross-cutting support for culture ministry: HR (11,000 agents), international cultural action, digital transformation, ministry operations, and DRAC coordination.
beneficiaries: Ministry of Culture staff, DRAC regional directorates

name: Transports aeriens
description: Civil aviation policy. Transferred to DGAC budgetary annex (612-614) and programme 203.
beneficiaries: Civil aviation sector, DGAC

name: Vie politique, cultuelle et associative
mission: Administration generale et territoriale de l'Etat
description: Elections, political party funding, religious associations. Split into programme 232 and programme 216.
beneficiaries: Political parties, electoral commissions

name: Vie de l'eleve
description: Supports student welfare: school health services, national scholarships (bourses), school social work, school climate improvement, and citizenship education (Pass culture).
beneficiaries: Primary and secondary students, school nurses, school social workers

name: Vie etudiante
description: Funds student welfare in higher education: CROUS scholarships (750,000 boursiers), 175,000 university housing units, RU student restaurants, and student health services.
beneficiaries: University students, CROUS, scholarship recipients

name: Surete nucleaire et radioprotection
description: Funds the ASN (Autorite de surete nucleaire) for nuclear safety regulation, radiation protection, and oversight of 58 nuclear reactors and other nuclear installations.
beneficiaries: ASN, nuclear facility operators, general public

name: Aide publique au developpement - coordination
description: Coordinated overall ODA policy. Functions absorbed into programmes 110, 209, and 384.
beneficiaries: Developing countries

name: Prevention de la delinquance et de la radicalisation
description: Delinquency prevention and counter-radicalization via CIPDR. Absorbed into programme 216.
beneficiaries: CIPDR, at-risk populations

name: Facilitation et securisation des echanges
description: Funds the DGDDI (Customs, 17,000 agents) for border control, trade facilitation, customs duties collection, anti-smuggling operations, and excise tax management.
beneficiaries: DGDDI (Customs), importers/exporters, border regions

name: Strategies economiques
description: Funds economic strategy through DG Tresor, Banque de France oversight, and France's contributions to international economic organizations (IMF, World Bank, OECD, G7/G20).
beneficiaries: DG Tresor, Banque de France, international economic organizations

name: Securite et protection des libertes
description: Cybersecurity (ANSSI) and digital security. Functions merged into programme 129.
beneficiaries: ANSSI, critical infrastructure

name: Administration territoriale
mission: Administration generale et territoriale de l'Etat
description: Territorial State administration. Restructured as programme 354 from 2020.
beneficiaries: Prefectures, sub-prefectures

name: Protection des droits et libertes
description: Funds independent administrative authorities: CNIL (data protection), Defenseur des droits (ombudsman), ARCOM (media regulation), CNCDH, HATVP, CNCTR, and CADA.
beneficiaries: CNIL, Defenseur des droits, ARCOM, HATVP, citizens

name: Entretien des batiments de l'Etat
description: Centralized State building maintenance credits. Transferred to programme 348 and CAS 723.
beneficiaries: State building occupants

name: Transmission des savoirs et democratisation de la culture
description: Cultural education and democratization. Renumbered as programme 361.
beneficiaries: Cultural education sector

name: Contribution de l'Etat a l'assurance maladie des fonctionnaires
description: State employer contribution to civil servant health insurance scheme.
beneficiaries: Active civil servants

name: Contribution de l'Etat au financement de l'assurance vieillesse
description: State employer pension contribution, transferred to CAS Pensions account.
beneficiaries: Active civil servants (pension rights)

name: Financement du numerique et des usages
description: Digital infrastructure and user services. Functions merged into programme 129 and 352.
beneficiaries: DINUM, digital services

name: Contribution de l'Etat a la couverture des risques sociaux dans les transports
description: State contribution to social risk coverage for transport workers under legacy schemes.
beneficiaries: Transport workers

name: Presence francaise a l'etranger
description: French cultural presence abroad. Merged into programmes 185 and 151.
beneficiaries: French cultural network abroad

name: Moyens mutualises des administrations deconcentrees
description: Shared resources for deconcentrated services (real estate, vehicles). Absorbed into programme 354.
beneficiaries: DDI, DREAL, prefectoral services

name: Conseil superieur de la magistrature
